trivia_bullets.text: The Theros Underworld — ruled by Erebos, God of the Dead — is defined throughout THB as an active, hostile domain rather than a passive resting place. The Wizards story summary for THB (Jan 2020) depicts Erebos as a jealous and tyrannical ruler who allowed the dead to escape during his centuries-long conflict with Heliod. Underworld Fires embodies this instability: its flavor text 'The Underworld is anything but a resting place' is a direct statement of the set's central narrative premise. [Wizards.com: Theros Beyond Death Story Summary, Jan 10 2020; Scryfall flavor_text: thb/162]
sources: Wizards.com: Theros Beyond Death Story Summary, 2020-01-10

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "The Underworld is anything but a resting place." Six words that frame the entire THB set premise: the Underworld is an active, torment-filled domain where the dead cannot escape Erebos's notice — except during the events of this set, when Erebos's distraction with Heliod lets souls slip free via the Escape mechanic. [Scryfall flavor_text: thb/162]

The exile rider on Underworld Fires — 'If a permanent dealt damage this way would die this turn, exile it instead' — is a thematic echo of the Escape mechanic's graveyard reliance. In THB's mechanical ecosystem, keeping creatures out of graveyards directly disrupts Escape costs. This creates an intentional tension: Underworld Fires hostes the Escape mechanic at the design level, reflecting the Underworld's function as a place where the dead should stay dead. EDHREC rank 23,012 and Pauper-ineligible status (uncommon) limit its adoption; it sees occasional niche play in formats where the exile clause matters against graveyard strategies. [Scryfall oracle_text: thb/162; Scryfall legalities field; Scryfall edhrec_rank]

Art by Bayard Wu, whose work appears across multiple Theros Beyond Death cards. Underworld Fires has received no reprints since THB (2020) — it remains a single-printing card, which combined with its uncommon rarity and exile-clause utility makes it a format-specific tool rather than a Commander or Limited staple. [Scryfall artist field: Bayard Wu; Scryfall prints search: oracleid ed3d113d]
sources: Scryfall prints search: oracleid ed3d113d (single print)
